preview.summary: Five-time champions Brazil face debutant-era Haiti, back at the World Cup for the first time in 52 years. Carlo Ancelotti's star-laden Seleção are heavy favorites against a resilient, diaspora-built Grenadiers side seeking their first-ever World Cup point.
preview.homeForm: Ranked 6th by FIFA and chasing a sixth title under Ancelotti, with Neymar restored to the squad.
preview.awayForm: Topped their CONCACAF qualifying group despite playing every match abroad; ranked 83rd, bottom of Group C.
preview.homeKey: Vinícius Júnior, Real Madrid's attacking reference and talisman.
preview.awayKey: Duckens Nazon, all-time top scorer who fired Haiti through qualifying.
storylines: Haiti's first World Cup since 1974, still chasing a maiden point
Ancelotti's blend of Vinícius, Raphinha and a returning Neymar
Premier League additions Isidor and Bellegarde bolster the underdogs
preview.h2h: Brazil overwhelmed Haiti 7-1 in their best-known meeting at the 2016 Copa América Centenario.
